The exemption will not frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act
The applicant must be based in an equivalent jurisdiction with authorization from the supervisory authority
The applicant must comply with any criteria prescribed in joint standards for exemption
The applicant must undertake to cooperate and share information with the Authority, South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority

sections.source_quote: The application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice; and the granting of the exemption will not— (aa) conflict with the public interest; or (bb) frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act; and the application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice.

It clarifies the Authority as the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established under the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines key regulatory entities and processes, which may indirectly affect data transfer assessments through regulatory oversight frameworks.

requirements.text: Authority means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established in terms of section 56 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
Conduct standard has the same meaning as in section 1(1) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
Register means the Financial Sector Information Register referred to in section 256 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
Tribunal means the Financial Services Tribunal established in terms of section 219 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act

sections.source_quote: ‘Authority' means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established in terms of section 56 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
sections.relevance_reason: Definitions of key terms for financial data compliance

sections.heading: Die indeling van artikels word
sections.title: Amendment of reporting standards for financial regulators
sections.summary: This section amends section 25(2) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act by substituting language to allow the Registrar Authority to prescribe standards for reports referenced in subsection (1). The amendment specifies that the Authority may prescribe standards for reports, including the conditions under which they must be issued. This affects the reporting requirements for financial entities regulated under the Act.

requirements.text: The Registrar Authority may prescribe standards in respect of reports referred to in subsection (1)

sections.source_quote: The amendment of section 25(2) by the substitution for the words preceding paragraph (a) of the following words: 'The [registrar] Authority may[,] prescribe standards in respect of [a report] reports referred to in subsection (1)[, prescribe] specifying—'
sections.relevance_reason: regulator powers for reporting standards

sections.heading: The insertion after section 1 of the
sections.title: Financial Sector Regulation Act alignment with other laws
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulation Act aligns with the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It requires that any provision of the Act that refers to a 'regulatory instrument' must be interpreted as a matter prescribed by the Authority where notice in the Gazette is specifically required. The section also specifies that references to inspections, prescribed fees, and reviews of decisions must be read as investigations, relevant fees determined under section 237, and reconsiderations by the Tribunal, respectively.
sections.dtia_summary: This section clarifies how the Financial Sector Regulation Act interacts with other regulatory instruments and procedures, which is relevant for DTIAs when aligning data transfer mechanisms with existing regulatory frameworks.

requirements.text: A reference in this Act to an inspection must be read as a reference to an investigation in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to the Authority publishing information on a website must be read as a reference to publishing in the Register
The Authority may also publish information or documents on its website
A reference in this Act to a prescribed fee must be read as a reference to the relevant fee determined in terms of section 237 and Chapter 16 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to a review of a decision of the Authority must be read as a reference to a reconsideration of the decision by the Tribunal

sections.source_quote: A reference in this Act to an inspection in terms of a provision of this Act must be read as a reference to an investigation in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulatory alignment and interpretation of terms

sections.heading: The amendment of section 21—
sections.title: Exemptions for market infrastructure operators
sections.summary: This section outlines conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators. Applicants must demonstrate that their operations do not conflict with public interest, frustrate the Act's objectives, or cause financial hardship to applicants or clients. The exemption process requires concurrence from the South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority, and includes specific criteria for external market infrastructure.
sections.dtia_summary: This section details conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements, which are critical for data transfer impact assessments involving market infrastructure operators. Exemptions require demonstrating no conflict with public interest, no frustration of Act objectives, and no financial hardship to applicants or clients.

requirements.text: The application of the section will not cause financial or other hardship to the applicant or clients
The exemption will not conflict with public interest
The exemption will not frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act
The applicant must be based in an equivalent jurisdiction with authorization from the supervisory authority
The applicant must comply with any criteria prescribed in joint standards for exemption
The applicant must undertake to cooperate and share information with the Authority, South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority

sections.source_quote: The application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice; and the granting of the exemption will not— (aa) conflict with the public interest; or (bb) frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act; and the application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice.
sections.relevance_reason: Exemptions require financial data compliance checks

sections.heading: The amendment of section 9—
sections.title: Delegation of powers and duties by Authority
sections.summary: This section allows the Authority to delegate powers and duties to individuals or entities for matters related to conduct standards. It requires that the delegatee has sufficient financial, management, human resources, and experience to perform the delegated tasks. Delegations are subject to limitations and conditions specified in the delegation document. Delegations do not divest the Authority of responsibility, and any actions taken by the delegate are deemed to be done by the Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines the conditions for delegating authority in financial services, which may impact data transfer processes if the Authority is involved in cross-border financial regulation.

requirements.text: The Authority may delegate powers or duties to any person for matters related to conduct standards
The Authority must be satisfied that the delegatee has sufficient financial, management, human resources, and experience
Delegations are subject to limitations and conditions specified in the delegation
Delegations do not divest the Authority of responsibility for the delegated power or duty
Actions taken by a delegate in accordance with a delegation are deemed to be done by the Authority
Delegations may be amended or revoked in writing at any time

sections.source_quote: The Authority may, in writing, delegate to any person a power or duty conferred upon the Authority under this Act in respect of any matter relating to a conduct standard referred to in section 6A(2)(a), (b) and (e).
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential governance for financial data delegation

sections.heading: The amendment of section 11 by the
sections.title: Supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulator (FSR) must establish supervisory cooperation with equivalent foreign authorities. It requires specific mechanisms for information exchange, breach notifications, coordination of inspections, and handling of supervisory information for market infrastructure entities. The cooperation must cover international market infrastructure licenses and ensure timely responses to regulatory concerns.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires international supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure, including information exchange protocols and breach notification mechanisms that impact cross-border data transfers and regulatory oversight.

requirements.text: The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for information exchange between the Authority, South African Reserve Bank, Prudential Authority, and relevant supervisory authorities
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for prompt notification to the Authority when a supervisory authority deems an external market infrastructure in breach
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for coordination of supervisory activities including cross-border on-site inspections
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify processes for using supervisory information for law enforcement purposes
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for cooperation including examination reports and document analysis
The Authority and supervisory authorities must establish confidential safeguards for non-public supervisory information
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share risk analysis assessments to identify and mitigate market risks
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share information on entities of systemic significance
The Authority and supervisory authorities must cooperate in routine oversight of internationally active licensed market infrastructure
The Authority must provide advance notification regarding issues that may affect regulatory interests of other authorities
The Authority must design mechanisms for supervisory cooperation during crisis periods
The Authority and supervisory authorities must maintain ongoing staff communications regarding international market infrastructure

sections.source_quote: 6C. (1) The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction for the purpose of performing its functions in terms of this Act.
sections.relevance_reason: Breach notification and cross-border cooperation

sections.heading: The amendment of section 26—
sections.title: Amendment of the arrangement of sections
sections.summary: This section amends the arrangement of sections by inserting items 1A and 1B, which define the relationship between the Act and the Financial Sector Regulation Act and regulatory instruments. It specifies that any matter prescribed by the Authority requiring Gazette notice is considered a regulatory instrument for the purposes of the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for DTIAs as it establishes how regulatory instruments are defined and their relationship to the Financial Sector Regulation Act, impacting cross-border data transfers involving regulatory compliance.

requirements.text: Insert item 1A: Relationship between Act and Financial Sector Regulation Act
Insert item 1B: Regulatory instruments

sections.source_quote: Amendment of the arrangement of sections by the insertion after item 1 of the following items: '1A. Relationship between Act and Financial Sector Regulation Act 1B. Regulatory instruments'.
sections.relevance_reason: pure amendment mechanics

sections.heading: The amendment of Schedule 1—
sections.title: Reporting requirements for cooperative banks
sections.summary: Section 50 requires the supervisor authority to mandate cooperative banks to submit specific information or reports to the authority upon written notice. Reports must be prepared at the expense of the cooperative bank, and may include auditor reports on matters specified in the notice.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates reporting obligations for cooperative banks, impacting data transfer assessments by requiring detailed financial disclosures.

requirements.text: Cooperative banks must submit information or auditor reports upon written notice to supervisor authority

sections.source_quote: The [supervisor] Authority may on written notice require a co-operative bank, a co-operative financial institution, a representative body or a support organisation [of a co-operative bank] to submit to [him or her] the Authority—
sections.relevance_reason: reporting requirements for financial data

sections.heading: The repeal of section 5.
sections.title: Licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators
sections.summary: This section mandates that persons operating as exchanges, central securities depositories, trade repositories, clearing houses, or central counterparties must be licensed under specified sections. Unlicensed entities cannot purport to be such entities or use names suggesting connections to licensed market infrastructure operators. The section defines prohibited activities for unlicensed persons to prevent market manipulation and ensure regulatory oversight.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators, which directly impacts cross-border data transfers by requiring licensed entities to handle financial data under specific regulatory frameworks. Unlicensed entities face restrictions on using names or descriptions that suggest market infrastructure connections.

requirements.text: A person who is not licensed as an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house or central counterparty
A participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities

May not purport to be an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities
May not behave in a manner or use a name or description that suggests, signifies or implies connection to licensed market infrastructure operators

sections.source_quote: A person who is not— (a) licensed as an exchange, a central securities depository, a trade repository [or], a clearing house or a central counterparty; (b) a participant; (c) an authorised user; (d) a clearing member; (e) an approved nominee; [or] (f) an issuer of listed securities, may not purport to be an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities, as the case may be, or behave in a manner or use a name or description which suggests, signifies or implies that there is some connection between that person and an exchange, a central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user or clearing member, as the case may be, where in fact no such connection exists.
sections.relevance_reason: Licensing for financial data entities

sections.heading: The amendment of section 9—
sections.title: Delegation of powers and duties by Authority
sections.summary: This section allows the Authority to delegate powers and duties to individuals or entities for matters related to conduct standards. It requires that the delegatee has sufficient financial, management, human resources, and experience to perform the delegated tasks. Delegations are subject to limitations and conditions specified in the delegation document. Delegations do not divest the Authority of responsibility, and any actions taken by the delegate are deemed to be done by the Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines the conditions for delegating authority in financial services, which may impact data transfer processes if the Authority is involved in cross-border financial regulation.

requirements.text: The Authority may delegate powers or duties to any person for matters related to conduct standards
The Authority must be satisfied that the delegatee has sufficient financial, management, human resources, and experience
Delegations are subject to limitations and conditions specified in the delegation
Delegations do not divest the Authority of responsibility for the delegated power or duty
Actions taken by a delegate in accordance with a delegation are deemed to be done by the Authority
Delegations may be amended or revoked in writing at any time

sections.source_quote: The Authority may, in writing, delegate to any person a power or duty conferred upon the Authority under this Act in respect of any matter relating to a conduct standard referred to in section 6A(2)(a), (b) and (e).

sections.heading: The amendment of section 10 by the
sections.title: Exemptions for market infrastructure operators
sections.summary: This section outlines conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators. Applicants must demonstrate that their operations do not conflict with public interest, frustrate the Act's objectives, or cause financial hardship to applicants or clients. The exemption process requires concurrence from the South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority, and includes specific criteria for external market infrastructure.
sections.dtia_summary: This section details conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements, which are critical for data transfer impact assessments involving market infrastructure operators. Exemptions require demonstrating no conflict with public interest, no frustration of Act objectives, and no financial hardship to applicants or clients.

requirements.text: The application of the section will not cause financial or other hardship to the applicant or clients
The exemption will not conflict with public interest
The exemption will not frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act
The applicant must be based in an equivalent jurisdiction with authorization from the supervisory authority
The applicant must comply with any criteria prescribed in joint standards for exemption
The applicant must undertake to cooperate and share information with the Authority, South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority

sections.source_quote: The application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice; and the granting of the exemption will not— (aa) conflict with the public interest; or (bb) frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act; and the application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice.
sections.relevance_reason: Exemptions require financial data compliance checks

sections.heading: The amendment of section 11 by the
sections.title: Supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulator (FSR) must establish supervisory cooperation with equivalent foreign authorities. It requires specific mechanisms for information exchange, breach notifications, coordination of inspections, and handling of supervisory information for market infrastructure entities. The cooperation must cover international market infrastructure licenses and ensure timely responses to regulatory concerns.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires international supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure, including information exchange protocols and breach notification mechanisms that impact cross-border data transfers and regulatory oversight.

requirements.text: The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for information exchange between the Authority, South African Reserve Bank, Prudential Authority, and relevant supervisory authorities
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for prompt notification to the Authority when a supervisory authority deems an external market infrastructure in breach
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for coordination of supervisory activities including cross-border on-site inspections
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify processes for using supervisory information for law enforcement purposes
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for cooperation including examination reports and document analysis
The Authority and supervisory authorities must establish confidential safeguards for non-public supervisory information
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share risk analysis assessments to identify and mitigate market risks
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share information on entities of systemic significance
The Authority and supervisory authorities must cooperate in routine oversight of internationally active licensed market infrastructure
The Authority must provide advance notification regarding issues that may affect regulatory interests of other authorities
The Authority must design mechanisms for supervisory cooperation during crisis periods
The Authority and supervisory authorities must maintain ongoing staff communications regarding international market infrastructure

sections.source_quote: 6C. (1) The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction for the purpose of performing its functions in terms of this Act.

sections.heading: The amendment of Schedule 1—
sections.title: Amendment to section 49 on penalty recovery
sections.summary: This section amends section 49 to specify that if a cooperative bank fails to pay an administrative penalty within the specified period, the supervisor authority may recover the penalty through civil action in a competent court. This ensures enforcement mechanisms for non-compliance.

sections.heading: The insertion after section 1 before
sections.title: Financial Sector Regulation Act alignment with other laws
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulation Act aligns with the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It requires that any provision of the Act that refers to a 'regulatory instrument' must be interpreted as a matter prescribed by the Authority where notice in the Gazette is specifically required. The section also specifies that references to inspections, prescribed fees, and reviews of decisions must be read as investigations, relevant fees determined under section 237, and reconsiderations by the Tribunal, respectively.
sections.dtia_summary: This section clarifies how the Financial Sector Regulation Act interacts with other regulatory instruments and procedures, which is relevant for DTIAs when aligning data transfer mechanisms with existing regulatory frameworks.

requirements.text: A reference in this Act to an inspection must be read as a reference to an investigation in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to the Authority publishing information on a website must be read as a reference to publishing in the Register
The Authority may also publish information or documents on its website
A reference in this Act to a prescribed fee must be read as a reference to the relevant fee determined in terms of section 237 and Chapter 16 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to a review of a decision of the Authority must be read as a reference to a reconsideration of the decision by the Tribunal

sections.source_quote: A reference in this Act to an inspection in terms of a provision of this Act must be read as a reference to an investigation in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act.

sections.title: Co-operative financial institutions: regulatory authorization scope
sections.summary: This section modifies the scope of regulatory authorization for co-operative financial institutions. sections.heading: The amendment of section 45—
sections.title: Central securities depository registration requirements
sections.summary: This section amends section 36 by requiring that securities held by a central securities depository must be registered in the name of the depository or its wholly owned subsidiary, unless they are bearer instruments, money market securities, or recorded in an uncertificated securities register as per the Companies Act. This ensures clear ownership and regulatory oversight of securities.

requirements.text: Any securities held by a central securities depository in its central securities account must, unless they are bearer instruments, money market securities or recorded in an uncertificated securities register in accordance with section 50 of the Companies Act and the depository rules, be registered in the name of that central securities depository or its wholly owned subsidiary
The registration must be approved by the Registrar Authority

sections.source_quote: The [registrar] Authority may [direct] determine that any securities held by a central securities depository in its central securities account must, unless they are bearer instruments, money market securities or recorded in a uncertificated securities register in accordance with section 50 of the Companies Act and the depository rules, be registered in the name of that central securities depository or its wholly owned subsidiary, as defined in section 1 of the Companies Act, and approved by the [registrar] Authority.

sections.heading: The amendment of the arrangement
sections.title: Amendment to securities interest provisions
sections.summary: This section amends subsection (3) of section 39 to define when an interest in uncertificated securities may be granted. The interest must be effective against third parties in relation to a central securities account or securities account where it extends to all uncertificated securities standing to the credit at the time of the pledge. The provision applies to depositary rules and covers the scope of such interests.
sections.dtia_summary: This amendment clarifies the conditions under which pledges of uncertificated securities can be effective against third parties, impacting cross-border transfers of securities interests where depositary rules apply.

requirements.text: An interest in respect of uncertificated securities may be granted where applicable and in the manner provided for in the depositary rules
The interest is effective against third parties in relation to a central securities account or securities account
The interest extends to all uncertificated securities standing to the credit of the relevant account at the time the pledge is effected

sections.source_quote: An interest in respect of uncertificated securities may be granted under this section, where applicable, and in the manner provided for in the depositor rules, and is effective against third parties, in relation to a central securities account or a securities account, where such an interest extends to all uncertificated securities standing to the credit of the relevant central securities account or securities account at the time the pledge is effected.
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential: securities interest rules not directly data protection

sections.title: Definition amendments for financial sector conduct authority
sections.summary: This section amends section 1 by inserting new definitions for 'Authority', 'conduct standard', 'Register', and 'Tribunal', while deleting definitions for 'deputy registrar', 'Financial Services Board Act', 'FSB official web site', 'prescribe', and 'registrar'. It clarifies the Authority as the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established under the Financial Sector Regulation Act.

sections.heading: The insertion after section 1 of the
sections.title: Financial Sector Regulation Act alignment with other laws
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulation Act aligns with the Financial Sector Regulation Act. It requires that any provision of the Act that refers to a 'regulatory instrument' must be interpreted as a matter prescribed by the Authority where notice in the Gazette is specifically required. The section also specifies that references to inspections, prescribed fees, and reviews of decisions must be read as investigations, relevant fees determined under section 237, and reconsiderations by the Tribunal, respectively.
sections.dtia_summary: This section clarifies how the Financial Sector Regulation Act interacts with other regulatory instruments and procedures, which is relevant for DTIAs when aligning data transfer mechanisms with existing regulatory frameworks.

requirements.text: A reference in this Act to an inspection must be read as a reference to an investigation in terms of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to the Authority publishing information on a website must be read as a reference to publishing in the Register
The Authority may also publish information or documents on its website
A reference in this Act to a prescribed fee must be read as a reference to the relevant fee determined in terms of section 237 and Chapter 16 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
A reference in this Act to a review of a decision of the Authority must be read as a reference to a reconsideration of the decision by the Tribunal

sections.heading: The amendment of section 15A—
sections.title: Licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators
sections.summary: This section mandates that persons operating as exchanges, central securities depositories, trade repositories, clearing houses, or central counterparties must be licensed under specified sections. Unlicensed entities cannot purport to be such entities or use names suggesting connections to licensed market infrastructure operators. The section defines prohibited activities for unlicensed persons to prevent market manipulation and ensure regulatory oversight.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators, which directly impacts cross-border data transfers by requiring licensed entities to handle financial data under specific regulatory frameworks. Unlicensed entities face restrictions on using names or descriptions that suggest market infrastructure connections.

requirements.text: A person who is not licensed as an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house or central counterparty
A participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities

May not purport to be an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities
May not behave in a manner or use a name or description that suggests, signifies or implies connection to licensed market infrastructure operators

sections.source_quote: A person who is not— (a) licensed as an exchange, a central securities depository, a trade repository [or], a clearing house or a central counterparty; (b) a participant; (c) an authorised user; (d) a clearing member; (e) an approved nominee; [or] (f) an issuer of listed securities, may not purport to be an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities, as the case may be, or behave in a manner or use a name or description which suggests, signifies or implies that there is some connection between that person and an exchange, a central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user or clearing member, as the case may be, where in fact no such connection exists.
sections.relevance_reason: Licensing for financial data entities

sections.heading: The amendment of section 99(1) by
sections.title: Supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulator (FSR) must establish supervisory cooperation with equivalent foreign authorities. It requires specific mechanisms for information exchange, breach notifications, coordination of inspections, and handling of supervisory information for market infrastructure entities. The cooperation must cover international market infrastructure licenses and ensure timely responses to regulatory concerns.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires international supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure, including information exchange protocols and breach notification mechanisms that impact cross-border data transfers and regulatory oversight.

requirements.text: The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for information exchange between the Authority, South African Reserve Bank, Prudential Authority, and relevant supervisory authorities
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for prompt notification to the Authority when a supervisory authority deems an external market infrastructure in breach
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for coordination of supervisory activities including cross-border on-site inspections
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify processes for using supervisory information for law enforcement purposes
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for cooperation including examination reports and document analysis
The Authority and supervisory authorities must establish confidential safeguards for non-public supervisory information
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share risk analysis assessments to identify and mitigate market risks
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share information on entities of systemic significance
The Authority and supervisory authorities must cooperate in routine oversight of internationally active licensed market infrastructure
The Authority must provide advance notification regarding issues that may affect regulatory interests of other authorities
The Authority must design mechanisms for supervisory cooperation during crisis periods
The Authority and supervisory authorities must maintain ongoing staff communications regarding international market infrastructure

sections.source_quote: 6C. (1) The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction for the purpose of performing its functions in terms of this Act.
sections.relevance_reason: Breach notification and cross-border cooperation

sections.heading: The amendment of section 115 by
sections.title: Compliance failure definition for regulatory actions
sections.summary: This section defines what constitutes a failure to comply with regulatory directions. It specifies that failure includes not meeting any direction, requirement, notice, rule, regulatory instrument, or regulation under the Act or the Financial Sector Regulation Act. This definition applies to entities subject to regulatory oversight.

requirements.text: Fails to comply with any direction, requirement, notice, rule, regulatory instrument or regulation under any provision of this Act or the Financial Sector Regulation Act

sections.heading: The amendment of the arrangement
sections.title: Amendment to section 10 for systemic risk reporting
sections.summary: This section amends section 10 to require that supervisory authorities must inform the Financial Sector Regulator of any matter that may pose systemic risk to financial markets as soon as they become aware of it. It also modifies the appointment process for clearing houses or central counterparties to ensure they are licensed under Chapter V.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires timely reporting of systemic risk events to the Financial Sector Regulator, impacting cross-border financial risk monitoring and market stability.

requirements.text: Supervisory authorities must inform the Financial Sector Regulator of matters that may pose systemic risk to financial markets as soon as they become aware
Clearing houses or central counterparties may be appointed by exchanges under Chapter V licensing

sections.heading: Regulatory instruments
sections.title: Regulatory instruments
sections.summary: This section defines regulatory instruments as existing rules made prior to the effective date and prudential, conduct, or joint standards made subsequent to the effective date. It applies to the definition of 'regulatory instrument' in the Financial Sector Regulation Act, ensuring clarity on which instruments are considered regulatory.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines regulatory instruments, which are critical for DTIA as they establish the scope of regulatory standards that must be adhered to in data handling and transfer practices.

requirements.text: Regulatory instruments include existing rules made in terms of section 46 prior to the effective date
Regulatory instruments include prudential, conduct, or joint standards made in terms of section 46 subsequent to the effective date

sections.source_quote: Regulatory instruments 1B. For the purposes of the definition of 'regulatory instrument' in section 1(1) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act, the following are regulatory instruments: (a) existing rules made in terms of section 46 prior to the date on which this section comes into effect; and (b) prudential, conduct or joint standards made in terms of section 46 subsequent to the date on which this section comes into effect;
sections.relevance_reason: defines regulatory instruments relevant to financial data compliance

sections.title: Relationship between Act and Financial Sector Regulation Act
sections.summary: This section establishes the relationship between the Financial Sector Regulation Act and the Financial Services Board Act, specifying that the Registrar and Deputy Registrar of Securities Services act as the Authority until the Prudential Authority is established, and that the Registrar of Banks exercises the powers of the Prudential Authority under certain conditions.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 5 by the
sections.title: Delegation of powers and duties by Authority
sections.summary: This section allows the Authority to delegate powers and duties to individuals or entities for matters related to conduct standards. It requires that the delegatee has sufficient financial, management, human resources, and experience to perform the delegated tasks. Delegations are subject to limitations and conditions specified in the delegation document. Delegations do not divest the Authority of responsibility, and any actions taken by the delegate are deemed to be done by the Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines the conditions for delegating authority in financial services, which may impact data transfer processes if the Authority is involved in cross-border financial regulation.

requirements.text: The Authority may delegate powers or duties to any person for matters related to conduct standards
The Authority must be satisfied that the delegatee has sufficient financial, management, human resources, and experience
Delegations are subject to limitations and conditions specified in the delegation
Delegations do not divest the Authority of responsibility for the delegated power or duty
Actions taken by a delegate in accordance with a delegation are deemed to be done by the Authority
Delegations may be amended or revoked in writing at any time

sections.source_quote: The Authority may, in writing, delegate to any person a power or duty conferred upon the Authority under this Act in respect of any matter relating to a conduct standard referred to in section 6A(2)(a), (b) and (e).
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential governance for financial data delegation

sections.heading: The insertion after section 40 in Chap-
sections.title: Exemptions for market infrastructure operators
sections.summary: This section outlines conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators. Applicants must demonstrate that their operations do not conflict with public interest, frustrate the Act's objectives, or cause financial hardship to applicants or clients. The exemption process requires concurrence from the South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority, and includes specific criteria for external market infrastructure.
sections.dtia_summary: This section details conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements, which are critical for data transfer impact assessments involving market infrastructure operators. Exemptions require demonstrating no conflict with public interest, no frustration of Act objectives, and no financial hardship to applicants or clients.

requirements.text: The application of the section will not cause financial or other hardship to the applicant or clients
The exemption will not conflict with public interest
The exemption will not frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act
The applicant must be based in an equivalent jurisdiction with authorization from the supervisory authority
The applicant must comply with any criteria prescribed in joint standards for exemption
The applicant must undertake to cooperate and share information with the Authority, South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority

sections.source_quote: The application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice; and the granting of the exemption will not— (aa) conflict with the public interest; or (bb) frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act; and the application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice.

sections.title: De-registration and winding-up procedures for cooperative financial institutions
sections.summary: This section outlines procedures for de-registration and winding-up of cooperative financial institutions, including repayment of deposits, judicial management, and court applications. It requires cooperative financial institutions to repay deposits upon de-registration and provides mechanisms for winding-up.

A cooperative financial institution that fails to comply with repayment directives is deemed unable to pay debts
The Authority may apply to a court for winding-up or judicial management of cooperative financial institutions
The Minister responsible for co-operatives may not order winding-up without the Authority's written concurrence

sections.title: Amendment to section 8 for application procedures
sections.summary: This section amends section 8 to specify that applications for exchange licenses must be made in a prescribed manner and contain specific information as determined by the Financial Sector Regulator. It requires publication of application notices in national newspapers and on the regulator's website, with specific procedures for public comments and objections to proposed exchange rules.
sections.dtia_summary: Specifies procedures for publishing exchange license applications and proposed rules, impacting transparency and public participation in financial market infrastructure licensing processes.

The Financial Sector Regulator must publish application notices in two national newspapers and on its website
Proposed exchange rules and listing requirements must be available on the regulator's website for public comments
The period for lodging objections to applications or rules must be specified
The Financial Sector Regulator must publish proposed exchange rules and listing requirements on its website

sections.summary: This section amends the arrangement of sections by inserting items 1A and 1B, which define the relationship between the Act and the Financial Sector Regulation Act and regulatory instruments. It specifies that any matter prescribed by the Authority requiring Gazette notice is considered a regulatory instrument for the purposes of the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for DTIAs as it establishes how regulatory instruments are defined and their relationship to the Financial Sector Regulation Act, impacting cross-border data transfers involving regulatory compliance.

sections.title: Amendment to section 64 for asset transfer regulations
sections.summary: This section amends section 64 to specify that assets and liabilities of amalgamating entities, including insurance, guarantees, and compensation funds, vest in the amalgamated entity or designated entity, ensuring continuity of obligations to clients.
sections.dtia_summary: This amendment ensures that data and financial obligations transfer seamlessly during entity amalgamations, critical for maintaining data transfer integrity and client protections.

requirements.text: Assets and liabilities of amalgamating entities vest in the amalgamated entity

sections.source_quote: all the assets and liabilities of the amalgamating entities (or in the case of a transfer of assets and liabilities, of the entity by which the transfer is effected), including any insurance, guarantee, compensation fund or other warranty owned or maintained by any of them to cover any liabilities of clearing members of independent clearing houses or central counterparties, authorised users or participants, as the case may be, to clients, vest in and become binding upon the amalgamated entity
sections.relevance_reason: Amends financial data transfer rules

sections.title: Cooperative banks' reporting obligations to supervisor
sections.summary: Section 47(1)(a) mandates cooperative banks and financial institutions to report specific information to the supervisor authority. The reporting must include details about financial activities and compliance with regulatory requirements. This obligation applies to cooperative banks and financial institutions operating under the Financial Sector Regulation Act.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 48—
sections.title: Amendment to section 11 for hearing procedures
sections.summary: This section amends section 11 to require that exchanges may take into account information obtained by the Financial Sector Regulator during supervisory visits or investigations when conducting hearings. It also specifies procedures for publishing amendments to listing requirements, including timelines for objections and publication of approved amendments.
sections.dtia_summary: Specifies procedures for incorporating regulatory information during exchange hearings and publishing listing requirement amendments with public objection timelines.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 49—
sections.title: Amendment to section 49 on penalty recovery
sections.summary: This section amends section 49 to specify that if a cooperative bank fails to pay an administrative penalty within the specified period, the supervisor authority may recover the penalty through civil action in a competent court. This ensures enforcement mechanisms for non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides a mechanism for recovering penalties through civil action, which affects data transfer assessments by ensuring accountability for regulatory breaches.

requirements.text: Supervisor Authority may recover administrative penalties through civil action if not paid within specified period

sections.title: Reporting requirements for cooperative banks
sections.summary: Section 50 requires the supervisor authority to mandate cooperative banks to submit specific information or reports to the authority upon written notice. Reports must be prepared at the expense of the cooperative bank, and may include auditor reports on matters specified in the notice.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 55 by the
sections.title: Amendment of reporting standards for financial regulators
sections.summary: This section amends section 25(2) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act by substituting language to allow the Registrar Authority to prescribe standards for reports referenced in subsection (1). The amendment specifies that the Authority may prescribe standards for reports, including the conditions under which they must be issued. This affects the reporting requirements for financial entities regulated under the Act.

requirements.text: The Registrar Authority may prescribe standards in respect of reports referred to in subsection (1)

sections.source_quote: The amendment of section 25(2) by the substitution for the words preceding paragraph (a) of the following words: 'The [registrar] Authority may[,] prescribe standards in respect of [a report] reports referred to in subsection (1)[, prescribe] specifying—'

sections.heading: Kwenziwa utshintsho kwicandelo
sections.title: Co-operative financial institutions: regulatory authorization scope
sections.summary: This section modifies the scope of regulatory authorization for co-operative financial institutions. It specifies that the Agency may delegate regulatory and supervisory functions to co-operative financial institutions under certain conditions. The rules must apply to co-operative banks, representative bodies, or support organizations, or be limited to specific institutions based on type or budgetary size.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines the regulatory scope for co-operative financial institutions, impacting how data transfers and processing activities are authorized within the financial sector.

requirements.text: The Agency may delegate regulatory and supervisory functions to co-operative financial institutions with the Minister's concurrence
Rules may apply to co-operative banks, representative bodies, or support organizations
Rules may be limited to specific co-operative institutions based on type or budgetary size
Rules may only apply to co-operative financial institutions in specific cases

sections.source_quote: by the insertion after paragraph (aA) of the following paragraph: '(aB) co-operative financial institutions, in order to perform the Agency's functions in relation to co-operative financial institutions, including regulatory and supervisory functions, as specified in terms of this Act, or which the Authority may, with the concurrence of the Minister, delegate or assign to the Agency;'

sections.heading: The substitution for section 77 of the
sections.title: Central securities depository operational requirements
sections.summary: This section amends section 29 by requiring that a central securities depository licence specifies the registered office and operational locations. It mandates that the depository may not operate at any other location without joint prior written approval from the Registrar Authority, Prudential Authority, and South African Reserve Bank. This ensures oversight of depository operations across multiple jurisdictions.
sections.authority: Registrar Authority, Prudential Authority, South African Reserve Bank

requirements.text: The licence must specify the registered office of the central securities depository in the Republic and the places where the central securities depository may be operated

The central securities depository may not be operated at any other place without the joint prior written approval of the Registrar Authority, the Prudential Authority and the South African Reserve Bank

sections.source_quote: The licence must specify the registered office of the central securities depository in the Republic and the places where the central securities depository may be operated, and that the central securities depository may not be operated at any other place without the joint prior written approval of the [registrar] Authority, the Prudential Authority and the South African Reserve Bank.

sections.heading: (1) It is an offence for any person
sections.title: Clearing house licensing requirements for central counterparties
sections.summary: This section amends section 110 by deleting subsection (5) and adding a new subsection (6). It requires clearing houses performing central counterparty functions to be licensed by the South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority by January 2022. The licensing process must be completed by December 31, 2021, with specific approval requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing timelines and regulatory approvals for clearing houses, which may impact data transfer assessments if they handle financial data across borders.
sections.authority: South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority

requirements.text: Clearing houses performing central counterparty functions must comply with regulations or standards
Until 31 December 2021, clearing houses must be licensed as either associated or independent clearing houses and approved by the Authority, South African Reserve Bank, and Prudential Authority
As of 1 January 2022, clearing houses must be licensed as both independent and central counterparties

sections.source_quote: a clearing house performing the functions of a central counterparty must comply with any requirements imposed by regulations or standards, and must— until 31 December 2021, be licensed as either an associated clearing house or an independent clearing house, and be approved by the Authority, the South African Reserve Bank and the Prudential Authority

sections.heading: It is an offence for any person in
sections.title: False information in registration applications
sections.summary: This section prohibits providing false or misleading information when applying for registration as a co-operative bank or financial institution. It requires that all information submitted must be true and not misleading in any material respect.

requirements.text: No person may provide false or misleading information in registration applications

sections.source_quote: It is an offence for any person in connection with an application for registration as a co-operative bank or a co-operative financial institution to provide any information that to the knowledge of such person is untrue or misleading in any material respect.

sections.heading: (1) Kusisenzo esikukona ukuba
sections.title: Fraudulent management of co-operative institutions
sections.summary: This section prohibits reckless management of co-operative banks or financial institutions with intent to defraud creditors. It targets individuals who knowingly benefit from or manage institutions recklessly for fraudulent purposes.

requirements.text: No person may manage co-operative banks or financial institutions recklessly with intent to defraud creditors
No person may knowingly benefit from or manage co-operative institutions for fraudulent purposes

sections.source_quote: It is an offence for any person to be involved in or take part in the management of a co-operative bank or co-operative financial institution while the business is carried on recklessly, with intent to defraud creditors...

sections.heading: [Any] Where a decision or other
sections.title: Administrative justice compliance for decisions affecting rights
sections.summary: When an administrative decision or step by the Financial Sector Conduct Authority or Agency affects another person's rights, it must comply with the Promotion of Administrative Justice Act, 2000, unless a different fair administrative procedure is specified in this Act or the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.authority: Financial Sector Conduct Authority or Agency

requirements.text: Must comply with Promotion of Administrative Justice Act, 2000

sections.source_quote: 82. sections.heading: The substitution for the long title of
sections.title: Long title substitution for Financial Sector Regulation Act
sections.summary: This section substitutes the long title of the Financial Sector Regulation Act with a detailed description of its objectives, including promoting social and economic welfare through banking services, developing cooperative banks, establishing regulatory frameworks, and providing registration and supervision mechanisms for cooperative financial institutions.
sections.source_quote: 25. The substitution for the long title of the Act for the following: 'To promote and advance the social and economic welfare of all South Africans by enhancing access to banking services under sustainable conditions; to promote the development of sustainable and responsible co-operative banks and co-operative financial institutions; to establish an appropriate regulatory framework and regulatory institutions for co-operative banks and co-operative financial institutions that protect members of co-operative banks and co-operative financial institutions; to provide for the registration of deposit-taking financial services co-operatives as co-operative banks and co-operative financial institutions; to provide for the regulation and supervision of co-operative banks and co-operative financial institutions; and to provide for the establishment [of co-operative banks supervisors and] a development agency for co-operative banks; and to provide for matters connected therewith'.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 1—
sections.title: Definition amendments for financial sector conduct authority
sections.summary: This section amends section 1 by inserting new definitions for 'Authority', 'conduct standard', 'Register', and 'Tribunal', while deleting definitions for 'deputy registrar', 'Financial Services Board Act', 'FSB official web site', 'prescribe', and 'registrar'. It clarifies the Authority as the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established under the Financial Sector Regulation Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines key regulatory entities and processes, which may indirectly affect data transfer assessments through regulatory oversight frameworks.

requirements.text: Authority means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority established in terms of section 56 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
Conduct standard has the same meaning as in section 1(1) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
Register means the Financial Sector Information Register referred to in section 256 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act
Tribunal means the Financial Services Tribunal established in terms of section 219 of the Financial Sector Regulation Act

sections.heading: Kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 1— (cont.)
sections.title: Definitions of market infrastructure and participant
sections.summary: This section defines 'market infrastructure' as licensed central counterparties, central securities depositories, clearing houses, exchanges, and trade repositories. It also redefines 'participant' to include authorized users, clearing members, nominees, and others who perform custody and settlement services under central securities depository rules.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines key terms for financial market infrastructure and participants, which may impact cross-border data transfers involving financial market entities.

sections.title: Definitions of authority and registrar
sections.summary: This section redefines 'Authority' to refer to the Registrar and Deputy Registrar of Securities Services until the Prudential Authority is established, and specifies that the Registrar of Banks exercises the powers of the Prudential Authority under certain conditions.
sections.dtia_summary: Clarifies the roles of regulatory authorities in financial markets, which may affect data handling and transfer compliance.

sections.heading: The insertion after section 1 of the
sections.title: Relationship between Act and Financial Sector Regulation Act
sections.summary: This section establishes the relationship between the Financial Sector Regulation Act and the Financial Services Board Act, specifying that the Registrar and Deputy Registrar of Securities Services act as the Authority until the Prudential Authority is established, and that the Registrar of Banks exercises the powers of the Prudential Authority under certain conditions.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 3—
sections.title: Amendment of section 3 for regulatory consistency
sections.summary: This section amends section 3 to specify that the Financial Sector Regulation Act prevails over other national legislation except for the Financial Intelligence Centre Act and the Financial Sector Regulation Act itself, and that powers or duties conferred by other legislation must be exercised in consultation with the Registrar Authority.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures regulatory consistency between financial laws and data handling practices, which may affect cross-border data transfers.
sections.relevance_reason: regulatory consistency for financial data

sections.heading: kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 4—
sections.title: Licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators
sections.summary: This section mandates that persons operating as exchanges, central securities depositories, trade repositories, clearing houses, or central counterparties must be licensed under specified sections. Unlicensed entities cannot purport to be such entities or use names suggesting connections to licensed market infrastructure operators. The section defines prohibited activities for unlicensed persons to prevent market manipulation and ensure regulatory oversight.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators, which directly impacts cross-border data transfers by requiring licensed entities to handle financial data under specific regulatory frameworks. Unlicensed entities face restrictions on using names or descriptions that suggest market infrastructure connections.

requirements.text: A person who is not licensed as an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house or central counterparty
A participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities

May not purport to be an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities
May not behave in a manner or use a name or description that suggests, signifies or implies connection to licensed market infrastructure operators

sections.source_quote: A person who is not— (a) licensed as an exchange, a central securities depository, a trade repository [or], a clearing house or a central counterparty; (b) a participant; (c) an authorised user; (d) a clearing member; (e) an approved nominee; [or] (f) an issuer of listed securities, may not purport to be an exchange, central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user, clearing member, approved nominee or issuer of listed securities, as the case may be, or behave in a manner or use a name or description which suggests, signifies or implies that there is some connection between that person and an exchange, a central securities depository, trade repository, clearing house, central counterparty, external central counterparty, external trade repository, participant, authorised user or clearing member, as the case may be, where in fact no such connection exists.
sections.relevance_reason: Licensing for financial data entities

sections.heading: Kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 6:
sections.title: Exemptions for market infrastructure operators
sections.summary: This section outlines conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements for market infrastructure operators. Applicants must demonstrate that their operations do not conflict with public interest, frustrate the Act's objectives, or cause financial hardship to applicants or clients. The exemption process requires concurrence from the South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority, and includes specific criteria for external market infrastructure.
sections.dtia_summary: This section details conditions for exemptions from licensing requirements, which are critical for data transfer impact assessments involving market infrastructure operators. Exemptions require demonstrating no conflict with public interest, no frustration of Act objectives, and no financial hardship to applicants or clients.

requirements.text: The application of the section will not cause financial or other hardship to the applicant or clients
The exemption will not conflict with public interest
The exemption will not frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act
The applicant must be based in an equivalent jurisdiction with authorization from the supervisory authority
The applicant must comply with any criteria prescribed in joint standards for exemption
The applicant must undertake to cooperate and share information with the Authority, South African Reserve Bank and Prudential Authority

sections.source_quote: The application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice; and the granting of the exemption will not— (aa) conflict with the public interest; or (bb) frustrate the achievement of the objects of this Act; and the application of the section will cause the applicant or clients of the applicant financial or other hardship or prejudice.
sections.relevance_reason: Exemptions require financial data compliance checks

sections.heading: The insertion after section 6 of the
sections.title: Supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Financial Sector Regulator (FSR) must establish supervisory cooperation with equivalent foreign authorities. It requires specific mechanisms for information exchange, breach notifications, coordination of inspections, and handling of supervisory information for market infrastructure entities. The cooperation must cover international market infrastructure licenses and ensure timely responses to regulatory concerns.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires international supervisory cooperation arrangements for financial market infrastructure, including information exchange protocols and breach notification mechanisms that impact cross-border data transfers and regulatory oversight.

requirements.text: The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for information exchange between the Authority, South African Reserve Bank, Prudential Authority, and relevant supervisory authorities
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify mechanisms for prompt notification to the Authority when a supervisory authority deems an external market infrastructure in breach
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for coordination of supervisory activities including cross-border on-site inspections
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify processes for using supervisory information for law enforcement purposes
The supervisory co-operation arrangement must specify procedures for cooperation including examination reports and document analysis
The Authority and supervisory authorities must establish confidential safeguards for non-public supervisory information
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share risk analysis assessments to identify and mitigate market risks
The Authority and supervisory authorities must share information on entities of systemic significance
The Authority and supervisory authorities must cooperate in routine oversight of internationally active licensed market infrastructure
The Authority must provide advance notification regarding issues that may affect regulatory interests of other authorities
The Authority must design mechanisms for supervisory cooperation during crisis periods
The Authority and supervisory authorities must maintain ongoing staff communications regarding international market infrastructure

sections.source_quote: 6C. (1) The Authority must enter into a supervisory co-operation arrangement with the relevant supervisory authority from the equivalent jurisdiction for the purpose of performing its functions in terms of this Act.

sections.heading: Kuchitjiyelwa kwesigaba 7—
sections.title: Amendment to section 8 for application procedures
sections.summary: This section amends section 8 to specify that applications for exchange licenses must be made in a prescribed manner and contain specific information as determined by the Financial Sector Regulator. It requires publication of application notices in national newspapers and on the regulator's website, with specific procedures for public comments and objections to proposed exchange rules.
sections.dtia_summary: Specifies procedures for publishing exchange license applications and proposed rules, impacting transparency and public participation in financial market infrastructure licensing processes.

requirements.text: Applications for exchange licenses must be made in the manner and contain information prescribed by the Financial Sector Regulator
The Financial Sector Regulator must publish application notices in two national newspapers and on its website
Proposed exchange rules and listing requirements must be available on the regulator's website for public comments
The period for lodging objections to applications or rules must be specified
The Financial Sector Regulator must publish proposed exchange rules and listing requirements on its website

sections.heading: The amendment of section 10—
sections.title: Amendment to section 10 for systemic risk reporting
sections.summary: This section amends section 10 to require that supervisory authorities must inform the Financial Sector Regulator of any matter that may pose systemic risk to financial markets as soon as they become aware of it. It also modifies the appointment process for clearing houses or central counterparties to ensure they are licensed under Chapter V.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires timely reporting of systemic risk events to the Financial Sector Regulator, impacting cross-border financial risk monitoring and market stability.

requirements.text: Supervisory authorities must inform the Financial Sector Regulator of matters that may pose systemic risk to financial markets as soon as they become aware
Clearing houses or central counterparties may be appointed by exchanges under Chapter V licensing

sections.heading: The amendment of section 11—
sections.title: Amendment to section 11 for hearing procedures
sections.summary: This section amends section 11 to require that exchanges may take into account information obtained by the Financial Sector Regulator during supervisory visits or investigations when conducting hearings. It also specifies procedures for publishing amendments to listing requirements, including timelines for objections and publication of approved amendments.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 12(6) by
sections.title: Amendment to section 49 on penalty recovery
sections.summary: This section amends section 49 to specify that if a cooperative bank fails to pay an administrative penalty within the specified period, the supervisor authority may recover the penalty through civil action in a competent court. This ensures enforcement mechanisms for non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides a mechanism for recovering penalties through civil action, which affects data transfer assessments by ensuring accountability for regulatory breaches.

requirements.text: Supervisor Authority may recover administrative penalties through civil action if not paid within specified period

sections.source_quote: If a co-operative bank or co-operative financial institution fails to pay an administrative penalty within the specified period the [supervisor] Authority may by way of civil action in a competent court recover the amount of the administrative penalty from the co-operative bank
sections.relevance_reason: Pure penalty enforcement mechanics

sections.heading: The amendment of section 25(2) by
sections.title: Amendment of reporting standards for financial regulators
sections.summary: This section amends section 25(2) of the Financial Sector Regulation Act by substituting language to allow the Registrar Authority to prescribe standards for reports referenced in subsection (1). The amendment specifies that the Authority may prescribe standards for reports, including the conditions under which they must be issued. This affects the reporting requirements for financial entities regulated under the Act.

requirements.text: The Registrar Authority may prescribe standards in respect of reports referred to in subsection (1)

sections.source_quote: The amendment of section 25(2) by the substitution for the words preceding paragraph (a) of the following words: 'The [registrar] Authority may[,] prescribe standards in respect of [a report] reports referred to in subsection (1)[, prescribe] specifying—'
sections.relevance_reason: regulator powers for reporting standards

sections.heading: The amendment of section 29—
sections.title: Central securities depository operational requirements
sections.summary: This section amends section 29 by requiring that a central securities depository licence specifies the registered office and operational locations. It mandates that the depository may not operate at any other location without joint prior written approval from the Registrar Authority, Prudential Authority, and South African Reserve Bank. This ensures oversight of depository operations across multiple jurisdictions.
sections.authority: Registrar Authority, Prudential Authority, South African Reserve Bank

requirements.text: The licence must specify the registered office of the central securities depository in the Republic and the places where the central securities depository may be operated

The central securities depository may not be operated at any other place without the joint prior written approval of the Registrar Authority, the Prudential Authority and the South African Reserve Bank

sections.source_quote: The licence must specify the registered office of the central securities depository in the Republic and the places where the central securities depository may be operated, and that the central securities depository may not be operated at any other place without the joint prior written approval of the [registrar] Authority, the Prudential Authority and the South African Reserve Bank.
